title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Automation and Data Client Systems · Mastery Check
summary: A production data release board evaluating a changed source contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for http caching, freshness, validators, vary, and invalidation, producing spec-aware cache decision notebook. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers spec-aware cache decision notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chang…
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Private Shared Cache
steps.instruction:
Order the Private Shared Cache Python HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

content.text: production data release candidate stores a response with max-age=50, ETag "rev-5", Age:5, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

hints: For Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Stale Revalidation Invalidation, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Stale Revalidation Invalidation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Stale Revalidation Invalidation, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: production data release candidate stores a response with max-age=20, ETag "rev-2", Age:2, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

For Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Stale Revalidation Invalidation, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Deleting a local entry guarantees every intermediary stops serving stale content. This remains false for Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Private Shared Cache to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Private Shared Cache.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using choose and revalidation plus specific case evidence.
